page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32 is a compute-focused instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 120 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 3.75 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in single-threaded and multi-threaded operations. The instance achieves top-tier results in text processing and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong performance in compression tasks, image processing, and static web serving. Standard operations, such as software compilation, ray tracing, and database tasks, fall within the average range. However, large-scale LLM text generation using a 70B model is a notable bottleneck, yielding poor performance due to the lack of hardware acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-32 offers a balanced profile for compute-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The inclusion of advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) enhances its utility for vector calculations and lightweight AI inference. This server is best suited for web serving, text processing pipelines, medium-scale database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32 is a compute-focused server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor on the x86_64 architecture. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance relies on its 16 physical cores and advanced instruction sets, including Intel AMX and AVX-512, to drive performance. Benchmark results show top-tier capabilities in text processing and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong performance in compression and static web serving. Conversely, heavy workloads like large-scale LLM generation perform poorly without dedicated GPU hardware. This server provides a cost-effective profile for compute-heavy tasks, making it highly suitable for web serving, text processing pipelines, and lightweight CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32 is a compute-focused server with 32 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering strong performance for text processing and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32 features 32 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 32-vCPU compute instance featuring Intel Xeon processors, optimized for text processing, compression, and lightweight machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 120 GB of RAM
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector math
Delivers top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM inference
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, limiting large-scale AI generation
Best suited for web serving, text processing, and CPU-based inference workloads
